page: Microsoft Azure D32als_v6 is a virtual machine instance belonging to the Dalv6 server family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features a dedicated AMD EPYC 9V74 processor utilizing the x86_64 architecture, configured with 16 physical cores and 32 vCPUs via multithreading operating at a clock speed of 3.7 GHz. The processor includes 32 KB of L1d cache, 1.0 MB of L2 cache, and 32 MB of L3 cache, with hardware support for advanced instruction sets such as AVX-512 and cryptographic acceleration via AES, VAES, and SHA_NI.

The instance is equipped with 64.0 GB of system memory, yielding a low memory-to-core ratio of 2.0 GB per vCPU. This allocation represents a specific hardware tradeoff, prioritizing raw compute density over memory capacity. The D32als_v6 does not include local storage, though it is premium storage capable, meaning external block storage must be attached for persistent data. It also includes zero complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

In benchmark evaluations, the D32als_v6 demonstrates strong single-core and multi-core performance. It achieves top-tier results in Geekbench single-core and multi-core tests, as well as in image processing, text processing, and Passmark CPU encryption. Multi-threaded bzip3 decompression and small-model LLM inference also rank in the top 25%. Conversely, memory bandwidth, database operations, and multi-core stress-ng performance fall within the average range. This profile makes the instance suitable for compute-heavy workloads with moderate memory requirements, such as software compilation, image processing, text processing, and small-scale machine learning inference.
description: The Microsoft Azure D32als_v6 is a compute-focused virtual machine from the Dalv6 family, powered by a dedicated 3.7 GHz AMD EPYC 9V74 processor. Built on the x86_64 architecture, it provides 32 vCPUs across 16 physical cores and features 64.0 GB of system memory, establishing a low memory-to-core ratio of 2.0 GB per vCPU. The instance lacks local storage but supports premium storage options, and it does not include complimentary public IPv4 addresses. Benchmark data highlights strong single-core and multi-core capabilities, particularly in Geekbench testing, image processing, and cryptographic operations, while memory bandwidth and database operations perform at average levels. This hardware profile is optimized for compute-intensive tasks with low memory footprints, such as software compilation, text processing, and small-scale language model inference.
og_description: Microsoft Azure D32als_v6 is a compute-focused virtual machine featuring a 32-vCPU AMD EPYC 9V74 processor and 64.0 GB of memory, optimized for CPU-heavy workloads with a low memory-to-core ratio.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure D32als_v6 is a 32-vCPU virtual machine powered by an AMD EPYC 9V74 processor with 64.0 GB of RAM and no local storage.
tagline: A compute-dense virtual machine featuring dedicated AMD EPYC processing and a low memory-to-core ratio for CPU-intensive workloads.
bullet_points: Dedicated AMD EPYC 9V74 processor with 32 vCPUs and 16 physical cores
64.0 GB of system memory providing a 2.0 GB per vCPU ratio
Strong single-core and multi-core performance in Geekbench and cryptographic tests
Premium storage capable with no local storage included
Well-suited for software compilation, image processing, and small-scale LLM inference
